summary: Fiery lead work, strong second‑set transitions, solid show overall
blurb: Jerry delivers fiery solos on Bird Song and Mississippi Half‑Step while Phil’s baritone vocals anchor the set. Highlights include a seamless China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider segue, Deal and an epic Morning Dew.
review: Seattle ’72 was a high‑energy outing that showcases the Dead in top form despite a few missing first‑set cuts on some releases. Jerry Garcia’s lead work is a standout – his soulful phrasing on Bird Song and the incendiary jams around Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo keep the band locked in, while Phil Lesh adds powerful baritone harmonies that shine through the entire performance. The second set flows beautifully; the classic China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider transition rolls into a punchy Deal before building to an emotionally charged Morning Dew. Bob Weir’s rhythm guitar rides high‑octane throughout, especially during his vivid Yellow Dog storytelling, and Keith Godchaux provides tasteful keyboard textures that complement the jams. Bill Kreutzmann’s drumming is tight and dynamic, giving the band a solid rhythmic foundation. The best soundboard capture (gd72-07-22.sbd.vernon.13562.sbeok.shnf) offers crisp clarity with only minor level quirks, making it the recommended listening source for this memorable Seattle show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Two recordings are clearly positive, praising Jerry’s lead work and standout songs (Bird Song, Mississippi Half‑Step, Morning Dew). Two others are mixed, noting level quirks and missing tracks but still acknowledging strong performances. The overall sentiment leans positive, yielding a modest +0.8 adjustment.

best_recording.reason: High‑quality soundboard capture with clear mix; includes most standout songs and minimal technical issues.

key_highlights: Jerry’s incendiary solos on Bird Song and Mississippi Half‑Step Uptown Toodeloo.
Seamless second‑set flow: China Cat Sunflower → I Know You Rider, Deal, and a soaring Morning Dew.
Bob Weir’s energetic Yellow Dog storytelling and solid rhythm work.
Soundboard recording offers crisp clarity despite minor treble boost.

band_performance.Jerry: Delivers fiery, heartfelt solos throughout – especially on Bird Song and the expansive Mississippi Half‑Step jam.
band_performance.Phil: Provides strong baritone backing vocals; shines on Bird Song, Mississippi Half‑Step, and adds engaging banter that lifts the show.
band_performance.Bob: Rhythm guitar is omnipresent and energetic; his vivid Yellow Dog story adds a memorable high‑octane moment.
band_performance.Billy: Bill Kreutzmann offers tight, dynamic drumming with full‑stop power that drives the jams forward.
